Partner workflow Referral and collaboration

PermitPulse for partners who need cleaner property-level permit research.

This page is for permit expediters, architects, designers, ADU consultants, investor-friendly real estate agents, contractors, and project managers who keep running into the same friction point: the address matters, the permit record is messy, and nobody wants to forward a chain of screenshots as the answer.

Talk partnership Send a sample property
Where it fits

PermitPulse is built to support the handoff moments partners already have.

Permit expediters

Use PermitPulse when the project needs a cleaner permit-history baseline before strategy, intake, or owner conversation.

Architects and designers

Use it to tighten existing-condition research before concept, remodel scoping, or a tricky takeover.

Agents, ADU consultants, and contractors

Use it when a client needs a more reliable permit picture before buying, pricing, or moving work forward.

Simple collaboration options

Keep it lightweight.

  • Send a sample property when you want to test the fit.
  • Use PermitPulse as a back-pocket research layer for clients who need property-level permit clarity.
  • Refer work when your team wants the record summarized but does not want to own the research step.
  • Coordinate on a repeatable workflow if similar properties keep showing up in your pipeline.
What partners usually need

A cleaner summary, not more portal sprawl.

The strongest fit is when you already have the address, the client context, and a reason the permit story matters. That is the point where a Permit History + Risk Report can remove drag from the rest of the workflow.

How to send a property

Three pieces of context are enough to start.

  • Property address.
  • What you need to know: quote, acquisition, owner review, design handoff, or project triage.
  • Deadline or urgency.
Send a sample property See sample report
